user_question: How many vacation days do new employees get in their first year?
system_answer: Full-time employees accrue 15 paid vacation days during their first year of employment.
chunks_related: Unused vacation up to a maximum of 5 days can be carried into the next calendar year; any balance above that cap is forfeited on December 31. Employees also receive 10 paid sick days per year, which do not carry over. Parental leave is 12 weeks of paid leave for the primary caregiver and 4 weeks for a secondary caregiver, available after 6 months of employment.

Accrual increases to 20 days after three years of service and to 25 days after seven years. Vacation accrues monthly, so a first-year employee earns 1.25 days per month. Unused vacation up to a maximum of 5 days can be carried into the next calendar year; any balance above that cap is forfeited on December 31.

All data is encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher and encrypted at rest using AES-256. Customer data is hosted in regional data centers; Enterprise customers can choose between United States and European Union data residency. Time Off and Leave Policies Full-time employees accrue 15 paid vacation days during their first year of employment. Accrual increases to 20 days after three years of service and to 25 days after seven years.

Managers receive an email and an in-app notification, and most requests are approved within two business days. Employees can cancel a pending request at any time, but an approved request must be canceled by a manager. Onboarding New Hires The onboarding workflow guides a new hire through their first days.

If a pay date falls on a weekend or a public holiday, payroll is processed on the preceding business day. Pay stubs become available in the Pay tab on the morning of each pay date. Employees can download a PDF of any pay stub and update their direct deposit details, which take effect on the next payroll cycle. Compensation changes such as raises require HR Admin approval and are reflected in the next full pay period. Time Tracking and Approvals

user_question: How do I enable Single Sign-On (SSO)?
system_answer: SSO is available on the Business and Enterprise plans. To enable it, an Owner uploads the identity provider metadata in Settings > Security > SSO. If SSO is enforced, password login is disabled for all members except the Owner, who keeps a break-glass password.
chunks_related: If the link expires, an admin can resend the invitation from the same screen. Deactivating a user preserves their historical records but immediately revokes their access. Single Sign-On and Security PeopleFlow supports Single Sign-On (SSO) through SAML 2.0 and integrates with Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, and Google Workspace. SSO is available on the Business and Enterprise plans.

SSO is available on the Business and Enterprise plans. To enable it, an Owner uploads the identity provider metadata under Settings, then Security, then SSO. When SSO is enforced, password login is disabled for all members except the Owner, who keeps a break-glass password in case the identity provider is unavailable. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is strongly recommended and can be required for all users by an Owner.

Enterprise customers can build custom reports and schedule them to be emailed automatically. Plans, Billing, and Support PeopleFlow offers three plans. The Starter plan costs $6 per seat per month and includes core records and time off. The Business plan costs $12 per seat per month and adds SSO, performance reviews, and integrations. The Enterprise plan offers custom pricing and adds data residency choices, custom reports, and a dedicated success manager.

Unused vacation up to a maximum of 5 days can be carried into the next calendar year; any balance above that cap is forfeited on December 31. Parental leave is 12 weeks of paid leave for the primary caregiver and 4 weeks for a secondary caregiver, available after 6 months of employment.

user_question: What does the Business plan cost and what uptime SLA does it include?
system_answer: The Business plan costs $12 per seat per month and includes a 99.9 percent uptime SLA.
chunks_related: Only the Owner can change the plan or update payment details, under Settings, then Billing. The Business plan includes a 99.9 percent uptime service level agreement (SLA), and the Enterprise plan includes a 99.95 percent uptime SLA with priority support. Standard support responds to tickets within one business day, while priority support targets a four-hour response during business hours.

Enterprise customers can build custom reports and schedule them to be emailed automatically. Plans, Billing, and Support PeopleFlow offers three plans. The Starter plan costs $6 per seat per month and includes core records and time off. The Business plan costs $12 per seat per month and adds SSO, performance reviews, and integrations. The Enterprise plan offers custom pricing and adds data residency choices, custom reports, and a dedicated success manager.

The Enterprise plan offers custom pricing and adds data residency choices, custom reports, and a dedicated success manager. Billing is monthly or annual, and annual billing includes a 15 percent discount. Only the Owner can change the plan or update payment details, under Settings, then Billing.

Standard support responds to tickets within one business day, while priority support targets a four-hour response during business hours. To reach support, open the Help menu and select Contact Support, or email support@peopleflow.example. Status and incident history are published at status.peopleflow.example. Data Export and Account Closure Customers own their data and can export it at any time.

user_question: How do I request time off?
system_answer: To request time off, open the Time Off tab, select the dates, choose the leave type, and submit the request. It will then be routed to your manager for approval.
chunks_related: Parental leave is 12 weeks of paid leave for the primary caregiver and 4 weeks for a secondary caregiver, available after 6 months of employment. To request time off, an employee opens the Time Off tab, selects the dates, chooses the leave type, and submits. The request is routed to their manager for approval. Managers receive an email and an in-app notification, and most requests are approved within two business days.

Time Tracking and Approvals Hourly employees track time using the Time Tracking feature, which supports a web timer, manual entry, and mobile clock-in. Timesheets are submitted weekly and routed to the employee's manager for approval. Managers can edit entries before approving, and every edit is recorded in an audit log.
